description: MemoryBench v2 dataset: 100+ memories for retrieval/isolation/context evaluation

user_a.description: ML researcher focused on LLM and retrieval

memories.content: Senior ML researcher at Stanford, specializing in NLP and retrieval-augmented generation
Prefers PyTorch over TensorFlow for all experiments
Always uses wandb for experiment tracking and logging
Must cite at least 3 related works from top-tier venues when writing papers
Improve dense passage retrieval with contrastive learning on MS MARCO
ColBERT v2 achieves MRR@10 of 0.397 on MS MARCO dev set
Hard negative mining with BM25 top-1000 gives better training signal than random negatives
Decided to use FAISS for ANN index instead of ScaNN due to better Python API
Late interaction models should outperform single-vector models on multi-hop queries
Training converged after 150k steps with learning rate 2e-5 and batch size 128
Query expansion using pseudo-relevance feedback improved recall@1000 by 8%
Experiment deadline is March 15th for EMNLP 2025 submission
Build a multi-agent system for automated paper review and meta-review generation
GPT-4 achieves 72% agreement with human reviewers on accept/reject decisions
Using chain-of-thought prompting reduces hallucinated review comments by 35%
Will use Anthropic Claude for review generation due to better instruction following
Multi-agent debate between reviewer agents should improve review quality over single-agent
OpenReview API provides structured review data with confidence scores for training
Develop efficient inference for mixture-of-experts models on consumer GPUs
Mixtral 8x7B uses top-2 expert routing with 46.7B total but 12.9B active parameters
Expert parallelism across 4 GPUs reduces latency by 60% compared to tensor parallelism
Chose to implement expert offloading to CPU RAM for single-GPU deployment
This paper proposes RAPTOR which recursively clusters and summarizes document chunks for tree-based retrieval
RAPTOR improves answer accuracy on QuALITY by 20% over vanilla RAG with flat chunking
This paper introduces self-RAG where the LLM learns to retrieve, critique, and generate with reflection tokens
Self-RAG's reflection mechanism is too expensive for our real-time use case, will not adopt
LoRA fine-tuning of the query encoder with rank 16 gives comparable results to full fine-tuning
Switched from AdamW to LAMB optimizer in January for better large-batch training stability
Document encoder frozen during first 50k steps then unfrozen for joint fine-tuning
Reviewer calibration dataset contains 2,500 papers from ICLR 2023 with 8,750 reviews
Aspect-based review scoring (novelty, clarity, soundness) correlates better with final decisions than holistic scoring

user_b.description: CV researcher focused on diffusion models and 3D vision

memories.content: PhD candidate at MIT working on generative models for 3D content creation
Prefers JAX and Flax for all diffusion model implementations
All experiments must be reproducible with a single A100 GPU within 24 hours
Accelerate diffusion sampling with consistency distillation for real-time image generation
Consistency models achieve FID 3.55 on CIFAR-10 with only 1-step generation
Progressive distillation from 1024 steps to 4 steps preserves 95% of image quality
Using LPIPS loss instead of MSE for distillation gives perceptually better results
Training the student model takes 80k steps with batch size 256 on 8 A100 GPUs
Generate 3D meshes from single images using score distillation sampling
DreamFusion uses SDS loss to optimize NeRF from a pretrained 2D diffusion prior
Multi-view consistency improves 3D quality but adds 3x compute overhead per iteration
Switched from NeRF to 3D Gaussian Splatting for faster rendering and training
This paper introduces DiT which replaces U-Net with a transformer backbone for diffusion models
DiT-XL/2 achieves FID 2.27 on ImageNet 256x256 class-conditional generation
